Moftabad](/wiki/Aliabad-e_Moftabad "Aliabad-

([Persian](/wiki/Persian_language "Persian language"): دهنوجهانگيرخان, also [Romanized](/wiki/Romanize "Romanize") as **Deh Now-e Jahāngīr Khān**; also known as **Deh Now** and **Deh Now-e Feyẕābād**)[[1]](#cite_note-1) is a village in [Azadegan Rural District](/wiki/Azadegan_Rural_District_(Kerman_Province) "Azadegan Rural District (Kerman Province)"), in the [Central District](/wiki/Central_District_(Rafsanjan_County) "Central District (Rafsanjan County)") of [Rafsanjan County](/wiki/Rafsanjan_County "Rafsanjan County"), [Kerman Province](/wiki/Kerman_Province "Kerman Province"), [Iran](/wiki/Iran "Iran"). At the 2006 census, its population was 45, in 11 families.[[2]](#cite_note-2)

Deh Now-e Jahangir Khan can be found at [GEOnet Names Server](/wiki/GEOnet_Names_Server "GEOnet Names Server"), at [this link](http://geonames.nga.mil/namesgaz/), by opening the Advanced Search box, entering "249375" in the "Unique Feature Id" form, and clicking on "Search Database".

["Census of the Islamic Republic of Iran, 1385 (2006)"](https://www.amar.org.ir/Portals/0/census/1385/results/all/08.xls) (Excel). [Statistical Center of Iran](/wiki/Statistical_Center_of_Iran "Statistical Center of Iran"). [Archived](https://web.archive.org/web/20110920084728/http://www.amar.org.ir/DesktopModules/FTPManager/upload/upload2360/newjkh/newjkh/08.xls) from the original on 2011-09-20.

**Deh Now-e Jahangir Khan** ([Persian](/wiki/Persian_language "Persian language"): دهنوجهانگيرخان, also [Romanized](/wiki/Romanize "Romanize") as **Deh Now-e Jahāngīr Khān**; also known as **Deh Now** and **Deh Now-e Feyẕābād**)[[1]](#cite_note-1) is a village in [Azadegan Rural District](/wiki/Azadegan_Rural_District_(Kerman_Province) "Azadegan Rural District (Kerman Province)"), in the [Central District](/wiki/Central_District_(Rafsanjan_County) "Central District (Rafsanjan County)") of [Rafsanjan County](/wiki/Rafsanjan_County "Rafsanjan County"), [Kerman Province](/wiki/Kerman_Province "Kerman Province"), [Iran](/wiki/Iran "Iran"). At the 2006 census, its population was 45, in 11 families.[[2]](#cite_note-2)
